toxicityDetail: Urushiol in all parts causes severe allergic contact dermatitis. Do not touch or burn.
Urushiol in all parts causes severe allergic contact dermatitis. Do not touch or burn.
Urushiol in all parts causes severe allergic contact dermatitis. Do not touch or burn.
Urushiol causes severe contact dermatitis. Among the most irritant plants in North America.
Coniine in all parts is lethal in small amounts. Resembles wild carrot and parsley.
Cicutoxin. Among the most violently toxic plants in North America; a small amount of root can be fatal.
Cardiac glycosides in all parts. Ingestion can cause fatal arrhythmia; smoke from burning is toxic.
Cardiac glycosides in all parts. Ingestion causes heart rhythm disturbance and can be fatal.
Tropane alkaloids. Berries are attractive and lethal, especially to children.
Ricin in seeds. A few chewed seeds can be fatal.
Aconitine in all parts, absorbed through skin. Handle with gloves.
Tropane alkaloids in all parts. Causes delirium; ingestion is frequently fatal.
Roots and mature stems and berries are toxic raw. Young shoots are eaten only after repeated boiling in changed water.
Berries and leaves cause gastrointestinal upset; sap causes contact dermatitis.
Berries cause vomiting and diarrhoea, particularly in children.
Seeds and pods cause severe gastrointestinal symptoms.
Bark, leaves, and seeds contain toxalbumins. Flowers are the only part traditionally eaten.
Raw berries, leaves, and stems contain cyanogenic glycosides. Berries are edible cooked.
Leaves, twigs, and seeds contain cyanogenic glycosides. Flesh of ripe fruit is edible.
Stinging hairs cause a painful transient rash. Cooking destroys the sting.
Skin and seeds should not be eaten; the flesh of ripe fruit is edible.
